mappings.rationaleAI: Directly covers photosynthesis as the production of food in green plants, including light-dependent reactions, Calvin cycle, chlorophyll, C3/C4/CAM pathways, and the Great Oxygenation Event — a precise match to outline path B.

Fungi are heterotrophic organisms with distinctive nutritional patterns including decomposition and mycorrhizal symbiosis with plant roots, directly relevant to nutritional patterns (A.1) and syntrophism/nutritional interrelationships (A.4).

Covers enzyme structure, lock-and-key and induced fit models, reaction kinetics, cofactors, and regulation — enzymes are the biological catalysts driving digestion in vertebrate digestive systems, relevant to the biodynamics and mechanics of digestion.

Traces the evolution of teeth from dermal denticles through mammalian tooth types (incisors, canines, molars), directly relevant to vertebrate digestive systems' oral cavity and teeth (C.2.b) and the evolutionary development of the vertebrate digestive system (C.2.c).

Examines the evolution of lungs and respiratory systems relevant to how organisms process nutrients through respiration, though the primary relevance is to vertebrate alimentary physiology — the vagus nerve controls digestive as well as respiratory function, connecting to vertebrate digestive system biology.

Parasitism represents a major heterotrophic nutritional pattern (A.1) involving methods of penetration and ingestion of host tissues (A.2), and parasitic strategies exemplify syntrophic relationships where one organism's metabolism provides nutrients for another (A.4).

Slime moulds challenge conventional classification and exhibit distinctive nutritional patterns that are neither purely autotrophic nor conventionally heterotrophic, relevant to the variety of nutritional patterns in outline path A.1.
